title: Glue Compressor
summary: Bus compressor modelled on the SSL G-Bus. Makes a drum group or mix bus feel like one instrument rather than separate elements. Not for individual tracks — for groups.
steps: Add Glue Compressor to a group track (e.g. DRUMS group), not an individual instrument track
Set Attack to 3ms — slow enough to let transients (the initial hits) pass through, then compress the body
Set Release to Auto — the compressor calculates release based on incoming audio
Lower the Threshold until the gain reduction meter shows 2–4dB of reduction on the loudest hits
Add Makeup Gain to compensate for any lost volume
Enable Soft Clip (the button at the output) for gentle limiting at the output — prevents the group from clipping
tips: 2–4dB of gain reduction is the target — you should barely hear it but the drums should feel more cohesive
Ratio 2:1 = very gentle glue. 4:1 = noticeable cohesion. 8:1+ = starts to sound like a limiter — usually too aggressive on drums

The difference between Glue Compressor and standard Compressor: Glue is modelled on an analogue SSL bus compressor with a specific 'glue' character. Use it on buses. Use standard Compressor for sidechain pumping on individual tracks
Try toggling the Glue Compressor on and off while the track plays — if the drums feel looser and more disconnected with it off, it's working

images.caption: Set Ratio to 4:1. Set Attack to 3ms. Set Release to Auto. Lower the Threshold knob until the GR (gain reduction) meter shows 2–4dB on the loudest hits. Enable Soft Clip (the button on the right side) to prevent clipping at the output.

ableton_version_note: Available in Ableton Live Standard and Suite.
